data.title: Construction Partners GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.05, revenue of $517.8M beats by $13.39M
data.description:
• Construction Partners press release (NASDAQ:ROAD): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $517.8M (+22.7% Y/Y) beats by $13.39M.

  • Record Backlog of $1.86 Billion

  • The Company is increasing guidance for fiscal 2024 with regard to revenue, net income, Adjusted EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA Margin, as follows:

    • Revenue in the range of $1.835 billion to $1.860 billion
    • Net income in the range of $73.5 million to $76.5 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $219 million to $228 million
    • Adjusted EBITDA Margin in the range of 11.9% to 12.3%

data.title: Berry Non-GAAP EPS of $0.18 misses by $0.02, revenue of $196.82M beats by $19.65M
data.description:
• Berry press release (NASDAQ:BRY): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.18 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $196.82M (-14.2% Y/Y) beats by $19.65M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $74 million in the second quarter, a 7% increase over the first quarter of 2024, with Cash Flow from Operations totaling $71 million and Adjusted Free Cash Flow of $19 million.
  • Produced 25,300 Boe/d, flat to first quarter; above the midpoint of 2024 annual guidance of 25,200 boe/d.

data.title: Rocket Lab stock soars on record quarterly revenue, constellation plans
data.description:
Rocket Lab USA (NASDAQ:RKLB) shares jumped over 15% Friday morning after the aerospace company reported record quarterly revenues, led by strong demand for its launch services and space systems products.

“This year’s second quarter was Rocket Lab’s highest revenue quarter in company history at $106M,” CEO Peter Beck said. That implies a 15% quarter-on-quarter increase and a whopping 71% year-on-year increase.

The Launch Services segment delivered revenue of $29.4M, slightly above the company’s guidance, while the space system segment delivered $77M in the quarter, also in-line with its guidance.

The firm ended the quarter with $1.07B of total backlog, with launch backlog of $294M and space systems backlog of $772.6M. “Our current backlog continues to support our current year target average revenue per launch of $7.5M, with some quarterly variability tied to volume purchase discounts, launch location, and mission assurance requirements,” the management said in the earnings call.

Looking ahead, Rocket Lab (RKLB) expects Q3 revenue of $100M-$105M (consensus estimate: $110.54M) and adjusted gross margin of 30% to 32%. Non-GAAP gross margin was 30.7% in Q2, in-line with the company’s prior guidance range of 30% to 32%.

Beck also shared a glimpse of where Rocket Lab is headed in the future, particularly plans or a Rocket Lab-owned and operated satellite constellation which is part of the executive’s strategy to become an “end-to-end space company.”

“By owning launch and spacecraft, we're at a distinct advantage when it comes to establishing our own space capabilities or constellations. We can build and launch our own spacecraft at cost and we don't have to wait in-line for limited launch capacity,” Beck said.

data.title: Sylvamo reports mixed Q2 results; initiates Q3 outlook
data.description:
• Sylvamo press release (NYSE:SLVM): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.98 beats by $0.40.
  • Revenue of $933M (+1.5% Y/Y) misses by $4.56M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $164 million (18% margin) vs. $118 million (13% margin).
  • Cash provided by operating activities of $115 million vs. $27 million.
  • Free cash flow of $62 million vs. $(33) million.

  • Third Quarter Outlook

    • Adjusted EBITDA of $170 million to $185 million
    • Compared to the second quarter:
      • Price and mix are expected to be slightly unfavorable up to $5 million due to mix
      • Volume is projected to increase by $10 million to $15 million, driven by Latin America and North America
      • Operations and other costs are expected to increase by $10 million to $15 million due primarily to higher unabsorbed fixed costs from economic downtime
      • Input and transportation costs are projected to increase by $5 million to $10 million due to fiber in Latin America and energy in North America
      • Total planned maintenance outage expenses are expected to decrease by $28 million

data.title: Enservco sells certain Colorado based assets of Heat Waves Hot Oil Service
data.description:
• Enservco (NYSE:ENSV) said it has sold certain Colorado based assets of Heat Waves Hot Oil Service to HP Oilfield Services.
  • The deal consideration was $1.7M, which included $1.2M in cash and a short term note of $473.4K.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds for retirement of debt, specifically borrowings under the company’s Utica Facility.

data.title: New Fortress Energy misses top-line and bottom-line estimates
data.description:
• New Fortress Energy press release (NASDAQ:NFE): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.41 misses by $0.47.
  • Revenue of $428M (-23.7% Y/Y) misses by $81.24M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $120 million in the second quarter of 2024.
  • Funds from Operations per share of $(0.23) on a fully diluted basis in the second quarter of 2024
  • FLNG 1 project complete with First Cargo expected in August 2024.
  • "For the full calendar year 2024 and 2025, we are forecasting Adjusted EBITDA of $1.4-1.5 billion, inclusive of the expected resolution of our outstanding early termination claims on our FEMA contracts, and $1.3 billion, respectively."

data.title: Legend Biotech GAAP EPS of -$0.05 beats by $0.21, revenue of $186.52M beats by $60.41M
data.description:
• Legend Biotech press release (NASDAQ:LEGN): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.05 beats by $0.21.
  • Revenue of $186.52M (+154.4% Y/Y) beats by $60.41M.
  • Cash and cash equivalents, deposits and short-term investments of $1.3 billion, as of June 30, 2024, which Legend Biotech (LEGN) believes will provide financial runway into 2026, when Legend Biotech anticipates achieving an operating profit

data.title: Solventum reports Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.56, raises full-year guidance
data.description:
• Solventum Corporation press release (NYSE:SOLV): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.56.
  • Reported sales increased 0.2% to $2.081 billion; organic sales increased 1.3%.
  • Generated $355 million in cash from operations; free cash flow of $297 million.
